problem: I have 6 shirts, 4 pairs of pants, and 6 hats. The pants come in tan, black, blue, and gray. The shirts and hats come in those colors, and also white and yellow. I refuse to wear an outfit in which all 3 items are the same color. How many choices for outfits, consisting of one shirt, one hat, and one pair of pants, do I have?

classified_questions.question: Can you describe the basic concept of counting in this problem?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What are the different options available for shirts, pants, and hats?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many choices do you have for each item individually?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many total outfit combinations can be formed by multiplying the choices of shirts, pants, and hats?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the condition given in the problem that restricts the outfit combinations?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many outfits have all three items in the same color?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can you calculate the number of outfits that do not have all three items in the same color?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why we subtract the number of outfits with all three items in the same color from the total outfit combinations?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What is the final answer to the question?
classified_questions.label: b
